commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Update README.md links
- Remove net API docs from memory
- The program doesn't use any network functionality
- Keep -ltracker for BFilePanel

commit.message:
Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

commit.message:
Classify UnpackedArrayConcatArrayExpansion → §10.10 [skip ci]

The test exercises unpacked array concatenation where array variables A and B (each int[0:1]) are expanded into a wider array C (int[0:3]) via `C = {A, B}`. The rule governing this element-wise expansion of unpacked array items is stated in the main body of §10.10 ("Unpacked array concatenation"). None of the subclauses 10.10.1–10.10.3 cover item expansion; they address comparison with assignment patterns, relationship with other constructs, and nesting, respectively.

commit.message:
docs(analysis): 添加故障容错系统性分析文档

推演 24 个复杂故障场景，验证 v2.5.0 可靠性和安全性：
- 网络故障（分区/中断/抖动）
- 节点故障（primary/worker/cproxy）
- 数据库故障（损坏/满盘/锁竞争）
- 并发冲突（配额/限流/熔断）
- 级联故障（雪崩/慢查询）
- 数据一致性（重复上报/缓存）
- 安全性（JWT/注入/DDoS）
- 边界条件（超大请求/时区）

结论：19 个低风险场景（79%），5 个中等风险（21%），0 高风险
系统生产就绪，建议实施 P0 改进（请求体限制/备份/监控）

Edit and Write for files not under any project directory.

  theme preference) as JSON to Google Drive's hidden appDataFolder

- Add bulk DAO methods (getAllSync, deleteAll, insertAll) across all 5 DAOs
- BackupManager handles serialization/deserialization of all Room tables

commit.message:
feat(auth): add logout button to admin sidebar and fix session cleanup

- AdminShell: add logout button in sidebar footer with LogOut icon
- logout/route.ts: preserve tenantId in /login redirect URL
- logout/route.ts: add line_tenant cookie to clear list (was missing)
- Logout flow: GET /api/auth/logout?tenantId=X → clear 5 cookies →
  redirect /login?tenantId=X → user can re-login with correct tenant

This ensures users with stale session cookies (tenantId:"default")
can easily log out and re-login to get a fresh session with the
correct tenantId.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Classify UnpackedArrayConcatScalarElements → §10.10 [skip ci]

The test assigns an unpacked int array from a brace-list of three scalar integer literals (`arr = {1, 2, 3}`). §10.10 defines unpacked array concatenation and its rule that an item whose self-determined type is assignment compatible with the element type represents a single element — exactly the construct exercised. The scalar-element rule is in the §10.10 body, not in subclauses 10.10.1–10.10.3.

- Simplify ProcessRunTab layout: compact form with collapsible Advanced section for timeout/maxOutputBytes
- Rewrite ProcessesTab: collapsible create form, lightweight list items with status dots, clean detail panel with tabs
- Extract error details: use problem.detail instead of generic "Stream Error" title for better error messages
- Fix GhosttyTerminal binary frame parsing: handle server's binary ArrayBuffer control frames (ready/exit/error)
- Enable WebSocket proxying in Vite dev server with ws: true
- Set TERM=xterm-256color default for TTY processes so tools like tmux, vim, htop work out of the box
- Remove orange gradient background from terminal container for cleaner look
- Remove orange left border from selected process list items
- Update inspector CSS with new process/terminal styles

- Add docs/processes.mdx covering the full process management API

commit.message:
fix: linked record columns reliably load data on all pages

Move prevIdsRef update to after successful batch fetch completion.
Previously, prevIdsRef was set before the async fetch started, so if
React re-rendered (strict mode or state changes during fetch), the
cleanup would cancel the in-flight request and the re-run would skip
because prevIdsRef already matched — resulting in empty linked columns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
